name: Freedom Flex

verdict.text: Great if you'll activate the rotating 5%; a chore otherwise

earn_rates.category: Rotating quarterly categories, activation required (up to $1,500/qtr combined, then 1%)

earn_note: The 5% rotating categories are the card's whole pitch, but they require quarterly opt-in (miss the activation window and you earn the base 1% instead) and cap at $1,500 in combined spend per quarter (max $75 back). 2026 categories: Q1 Norwegian Cruise Line/dining, Q2 Amazon/Chase Travel, Q3 gas/transit/EV charging/select live entertainment, Q4 TBD.

redemption_options.method: Statement credit / direct deposit / cash back

points.note: Technically Ultimate Rewards points, not a distinct cash-back currency; on their own they're worth a flat 1¢ each. Pairing this account with a Chase Sapphire or Ink Business card lets the combined points inherit that card's full redemption value, including airline/hotel transfers.

transfer_partners.highlight: Not a standalone transfer currency on its own; see the points note above on pairing with a premium Chase card.

credits.name: DoorDash DashPass
credits.subtitle: 6 mo free, then $10/quarter promo
credits.description: 6 months of complimentary DashPass membership ($0 delivery fees on DoorDash/Caviar), which then auto-renews at the regular paid rate unless canceled. Separately, up to $10/quarter in statement credits on non-restaurant (grocery/retail) DoorDash orders through Dec 2027.
tips: Cancel DashPass before the 6-month free trial ends if you don't want to start paying for it; it auto-enrolls at the regular rate.
The $10/quarter non-restaurant credit is the only ongoing recurring value here; the free DashPass trial is one-time.

insurance.detail: Through Sep 19, 2026 only: up to $800/claim, $1,000/yr, 2 claims/yr, $50 deductible. Pay your monthly wireless bill with this card to enroll. Benefit ends Sep 20, 2026.
+1 year (on warranties ≤3 years, up to 4 years total)

protection_note: Currently the strongest no-fee Chase card for cell phone protection, but that benefit is being discontinued on Sep 20, 2026. After that date this card's protections are basically identical to the fee-free Freedom Unlimited. Trip cancellation and purchase protection are modest, and rental coverage is secondary only.
rental_note: Secondary coverage only: it pays after your personal auto insurance, covering what's left. Use a primary-coverage card, like the Sapphire Preferred, for rentals when possible.

services.name: Foreign transaction fee, being eliminated
services.detail: Currently 3% on purchases outside the US, but Chase is removing this fee entirely starting Sep 20, 2026, the same date cell phone protection ends.

services.name: Lyft bonus
services.detail: 2% total cash back on Lyft rides through Sep 30, 2027 (an extra 1% on top of the 1% base).

services.name: Chase Pay Over Time
services.detail: Option to finance eligible purchases of $100+ at a fixed APR/fee instead of paying the revolving rate.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards

benefits.text: Earns rewards on their spend (into your account)
Same purchase protection as the primary card

additional_cards.note: No cost to add users, but remember only one activation covers the whole account for the rotating 5% categories each quarter.

timeline.text: Complimentary cell phone protection is discontinued (losses after Sep 19, 2026 aren't covered). Foreign transaction fees are removed the same day, so this is a mixed change, not a pure cut.
The Lyft 2% total cash back bonus is scheduled to expire; the DoorDash $10/quarter non-restaurant credit expires Dec 31, 2027.
Freedom Flex launches as a new Mastercard World Elite product alongside a refreshed Freedom Unlimited, introducing rotating 5% categories plus 3% dining/drugstore and cell phone protection.
